metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5005
metadata.comment:
Rationale: "Strikingly, the added "new" synapses that were induced by the deletion of Nrxn2 were more powerful and had a higher release probability than Nrxn2-containing synapses. The increase in release probability was again documented by three approaches: a decreased paired-pulse ratio (PPR) (Fig. 3E), a decreased coefficient of variation of synaptic responses (Fig. 3, F and J), and an enhanced inactivation rate of synaptic responses in the presence of MK-801 (Fig. 3, K to M)."

Authors note that this is unexpected and in contrast to Nrxn2's well studied family members Nrxn1 and Nrxn3, where deletions cause impairments in synaptic transmission in both mouse and human neurons (e.g. PMID:26279266, PMID:26030848).. Experimental description: Evidence for species, from main text: "Thus, we generated Nrxn2 conditional KO (cKO) mice that enable the deletion of all Nrxn2 isoforms."

The Nrxn2 conditional knockout, described in Figure 1a, affects both Nrxn2-alpha and Nrxn2-beta;
"To examine the function of Nrxn2, we generated cKO and constitutive KO mice that delete expression of all functional Nrxn2 transcripts (Fig. 1A), in contrast to the constitutive Nrxn2α KO mice that we reported earlier, and that do not delete Nrxn2β expression (32)."
